channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump’s Huge Windfall Has Few Known Global Precedents
item.description: President Trump’s earnings in office are at a level once unimaginable for any leader of a liberal democracy, particularly a sitting American president.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump disclosed that he has made immense profits since returning to the presidency.

item.title: MAGA Base Stays Quiet After Trump Reports Billions in Personal Gains
item.description: A new mandatory disclosure revealed that the president has earned $2.2 billion during the first year back in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Emily Davies
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: Some supporters of President Trump described his earnings as a validation of the business acumen they have long admired in him.

item.title: JD Vance and Melania Trump Also Had Sharp Earnings Increases in 2025
item.description: But the revenue brought in by the vice president and the first lady paled in comparison to that of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Fuller
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times; Allison Robbert for The New York Times
item.media:description: First Lady Melania Trump and Vice President JD Vance

item.title: As Ukraine War Escalates, Witkoff and Kushner Are Focused on Iran
item.description: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are pivotal players at a moment when the posts of U.S. ambassador to Moscow and Kyiv are both vacant.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Steve Witkoff, a White House special envoy, has met with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia seven times since President Trump returned to office.

item.title: Long Lines for Gas Shatter the Illusion of Normalcy in Wartime Russia
item.description: “Are we in the Soviet Union now?” asked one Russian, stunned and frustrated by the waits at the pumps.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva
item.media:credit: Alexander Nemenov/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Lines at a gas station in Moscow on Tuesday. Attacks on Russian oil infrastructure have caused the kinds of gas shortages that many Russian citizens have not seen in their lifetimes.

item.title: With Attacks on Crimea, Ukraine Hopes to Pressure Putin to End the War
item.description: Escalating drone strikes, fuel shortages and power cuts in the region Russia annexed are among the factors turning up the heat on President Vladimir V. Putin.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne and Nataliya Vasilyeva
item.media:credit: Alexey Pavlishak/Reuters
item.media:description: Yevpatoriya, Crimea, last month. The Black Sea region, which Russia annexed from Ukraine in 2014, has become a flashpoint in the war.

item.title: Blistering Heat Expected to Persist Into the Fourth of July
item.description: While relief could come to the Great Lakes and the far Northeast over the weekend, the Mid-Atlantic and Southeast will stay hot.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff and Mark Walker
item.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
item.media:description: Scorching temperatures will continue through the weekend. Most areas will cool a few degrees by Sunday.

item.title: Without Climate Change, U.S. Heat Wave Called ‘Virtually Impossible’
item.description: Scientists have said the conditions are the result of a climate that is “fundamentally different” from the time before fossil fuel use started rapidly warming the world.
item.dc:creator: Raymond Zhong
item.media:credit: Charly Triballeau/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Cooling off in New York City on Thursday.

item.title: NYC Hits 100 Degrees, Its Hottest Day in Nearly 14 Years
item.description: The last time the park recorded 100 degrees was July 18, 2012.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy
item.media:credit: Timothy A. Clary/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A thermometer at the weather station in Central Park reached 100 degrees on Thursday, the first such reading in almost 14 years.

item.title: On Eve of Taylor Swift’s Wedding, Rehearsal Dinner Kicks Off Weekend of Events
item.description: The event for about 100 guests at Madison Square Garden kicked off a weekend of celebration for the pop star and the athlete, as celebrities arrived in town and reinforcements were delivered.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tent structures were put in place, security was beefed up, and streets were closed as the couple prepared for their mega celebrations.

item.title: What Does It Cost to Take Over Madison Square Garden?
item.description: It’s not just rent. There’s security, insurance, permits and other costs, not to mention extra police presence.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta, Ishani Desai, Maria Cramer and Maia Coleman
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: Preparations at Madison Square Garden on Thursday.

item.title: Mamdani Is a Taylor Swift Fan. Here Are His Top 12 Songs.
item.description: Ahead of Taylor Swift’s wedding celebration in Manhattan, Mayor Zohran Mamdani revealed that he is a bigger fan of her music than previously known.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons
item.media:credit: REUTERS/Jennifer Gauthie
item.media:description: The wedding celebration of Taylor Swift and her fiancé Travis Kelce is set to take place on Friday at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding: What We Know So Far
item.description: The couple has revealed very little about their wedding plans, but that hasn’t stopped fans from searching for clues.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Photograph by Julio Cortez/Associated Press
item.media:description: After months of speculation, the couple confirmed their engagement via Instagram in a joint post last August. They began dating in July 2023.

item.title: The Perishable, Never-Ending Genius of Lionel Messi
item.description: How are his legions of fans savoring what could be the last World Cup of their idol? I dove into a sea of screaming Messis to find out.
item.dc:creator: Sam Anderson and Sara Messinger
item.media:description: Lionel Messi after scoring his second goal of the game against Austria in Dallas on June 22.

item.title: Allies Converge on Iran as Funeral Ceremonies for Supreme Leader Begin
item.description: Foreign dignitaries from China, Russia, Pakistan and elsewhere are set to pay respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Friday as days of ceremonies get underway.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Dignitaries walking past the coffins of Iran’s slain supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and his family members after paying their respects at the Grand Mosalla in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: U.S. Officials Believed Israel Was Plotting to Kill Iranian Negotiators
item.description: Any Israeli attempt to kill Abbas Araghchi, the foreign minister, or Mohammad Ghalibaf, the Parliament speaker, would have derailed peace talks, American officials feared.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Urs Flueeler
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, left, with Abbas Araghchi, center, traveled to Switzerland in June for a second in-person meeting with Vice President JD Vance and the American delegation.

item.title: Why One Global Shipping Choke Point Isn’t a Blueprint for Another
item.description: The Strait of Malacca may be a model for how Oman and Iran could collect fees in the Strait of Hormuz, but the differences between the waterways are vast.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee and Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Cheng Yiheng/Xinhua, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Some 100,000 vessels pass through the Strait of Malacca a year.

item.title: Has the MAHA Movement Given Up?
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his allies promised public-health libertarianism. The idea couldn’t survive once they took power.
item.dc:creator: David Wallace-Wells
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

item.title: David Hearn, Olympian Canoeist, Is Indicted After Arrest at Trump’s Reflecting Pool
item.description: David Hearn, a former canoeist, is accused of vandalizing the algae-challenged pool, which President Trump has said he would repair and beautify with fresh sealant. But the algae returned and the paint peeled.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: M. David Leeds/Getty Images
item.media:description: In 2000, David Hearn was one of America’s top competitive canoeists.

item.title: Man Rescued 8 Days After Quake, a Ray of Joy in Stricken Venezuela
item.description: The 44-year-old security guard was pulled alive from a pancaked basement, offering a fleeting moment of hope amid a soaring death toll.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Sulbarán, Shawn Hubler and Genevieve Glatsky
item.media:credit: Fernando Vergara/Associated Press
item.media:description: Rescue workers attend to Hernán Alberto Gil Flores after he was pulled from the rubble eight days after he was trapped by twin earthquakes that struck Catia La Mar, Venezuela, on Thursday.

item.title: Tell Us About How You Pursue Happiness
item.description: As America nears its 250th year, we want to hear from you about this particular phrase in the country’s founding document.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas
item.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times

item.title: How Communities Across the U.S. Are Ringing in America’s 250th
item.description: Plans include an air show in Texas, 18,500 fireworks in Idaho and a frog-jumping contest in Missouri.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto and Derrick Bryson Taylor
item.media:credit: Ethan Miller/Getty Images
item.media:description: A 300-foot-wide by 150-foot-tall American flag suspended across the Hoover Dam.

item.title: A Big Gamble for the Left: Can Socialism Appeal in a Swing State?
item.description: Francesca Hong, a state legislator running for governor of Wisconsin, wants to prove that a democratic socialist can win in a battleground. Some say she would hand the race to Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning and Jenn Ackerman
item.media:credit: Jenn Ackerman for The New York Times

item.title: F.B.I. Assigns Scores of Analysts to Examine Election Records in Georgia
item.description: The expansion of an investigation into Fulton County’s election office reflects President Trump’s desire to prove his baseless claims that the 2020 election in Georgia was rigged.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti, Alan Feuer, Dustin Volz and Richard Fausset
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: A memo says that the surge is part of a priority effort by Kash Patel, the director of the F.B.I.

item.title: Texas Governor Appoints Former Hard Right Critic to Key Post
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott appointed a firebrand former opponent, Don Huffines, to be state comptroller, underscoring a G.O.P. push for unity ahead of the midterms.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
item.media:description: Don Huffines at a forum featuring gubernatorial candidates running to unseat Gov. Greg Abbott in 2022.

item.title: How Public Pools Create Summer Communities in New York
item.description: Dry Dock Pool in Lower Manhattan is one of New York City’s 51 free outdoor pools where more than one million New Yorkers cool off with swimming, sunblock and community.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hu and Lexi Parra

item.title: This Pathetic Groveling Is No Way to Rebuild a Party
item.description: The Democrats’ constant solicitations for cash do not make their supporters feel like they are on a winning team.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Cottle
item.media:credit: Max Slobodda for The New York Times

item.title: The America That’s Still Possible
item.description: On America’s 250th anniversary, Bryan Stevenson, a civil rights lawyer and the founder of the Equal Justice Initiative, looks toward the work that is ahead for the country.
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein and Annie Galvin
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Happy 250th, America
item.description: A special newsletter to mark the semiquincentennial.
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: A Hitler Bunker Faces Demolition In Housing-Challenged Berlin
item.description: Preservation experts say the move to develop the site reflects a tension in Germany between the need to preserve historically significant monuments and the need to build and modernize.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Reiner Janick, via Berlin Underworlds Association
item.media:description: The New Reich Chancellery bunker.
